title: [Cmd Pal][PerfMon] Network speed formatters lack unit tests and duplicate scaling logic (follow-up to #49071)
author: HoneyTyagii
body:
### Summary

Follow-up to #49071. The Command Palette **Performance Monitor** extension formats network speeds with three near-identical scaling ladders that have **no unit-test coverage**. This is the exact code path where the #49071 bug (decimal units divided by `1024` instead of `1000`, so "1.0 MB/s" was really shown at 1,048,576 B/s) was able to ship unnoticed.

The divisor bug is now fixed, but the underlying risk remains: the logic is copy-pasted three times with hand-tuned magic thresholds and nothing guards the boundaries against regressions.

### Where

`src/modules/cmdpal/ext/Microsoft.CmdPal.Ext.PerformanceMonitor/PerformanceWidgetsPage.cs`

`SystemNetworkUsageWidgetPage` contains three private formatters selected by `SpeedToString` based on `NetworkSpeedUnit`:

- `FormatAsBitsPerSecString`   — Kbps / Mbps / Gbps, `/1000`, rollover at `< 999.5f`
- `FormatAsBytesPerSecString`  — KB/s / MB/s / GB/s, `/1000`, rollover at `< 999.5f`
- `FormatAsBinaryBytesPerSecString` — KiB/s / MiB/s / GiB/s, `/1024`, rollover at `< 1023.5f`

All three repeat the same three-tier structure (base → mid → top), the same `< 100 ? "{0:0.0}" : "{0:0}"` precision rule, and their own hardcoded rollover threshold. A one-character divisor mistake in any tier silently produces wrong values, which is precisely what happened in #49071.

### Why it matters

- There is no test project for the PerformanceMonitor extension and no test references `FormatAs*` / `SpeedToString` / `NetworkSpeedUnit`, so unit conversions are entirely unverified.
- The three thresholds (`999.5f`, `999.5f`, `1023.5f`) and divisors (`1000` vs `1024`) are easy to get out of sync, as #49071 demonstrated.
- These strings are user-facing in both the main list page and the dock bands.

### Proposed change

1. Add unit tests for the formatters covering:
   - Base-unit values (e.g. `0`, small B/s)
   - Rollover boundaries (values just below/at/above `999.5` KB and `1023.5` KiB) to lock in the "don't display `1000 KB/s`" behavior
   - Correct prefix at each tier for all three `NetworkSpeedUnit` options
   - Decimal-precision switch at `100`
2. Optionally collapse the three ladders into one shared helper parameterized by `(divisor, rolloverThreshold, unitSuffixes[])` so the SI-vs-IEC difference lives in one place.

This is a maintainability / test-coverage follow-up, not a new user-facing regression.

### Environment

Command Palette (Performance Monitor extension), current `main`. Applies to all platforms.

title: Copy as Plain Text
author: JGSstudios
body:
### Description of the new feature / enhancement

Copies text as plain text and removes plain text formatting.

### Scenario when this would be used?

Sometimes, plain text IS formatting, which can cause problems when using "Paste as Plain Text" because it doesn't remove the plain text formatting. I was pasting from Copilot, and using the plain text paste, Libre Office Writer (Word not tested) formats it with the plain text formatting, so I have to use the in-program function to actually paste as plain text.

### Supporting information

_No response_

title: Dock/Material/Transparent black font text
author: danagun
body:
### Description of the new feature / enhancement

Is it technically possible for the text color in Dock/MaterialStatus to automatically adjust to what provides the greatest contrast against the background? Could you try it and see if this is aesthetically sensible and beneficial?  If it is, then it would be great to offer user control through an enable/disable switch. Assumption: there exists an algorithm to compute the color with the greatest contrast to another color. 

### Scenario when this would be used?

If I were or am a power user, I usually try to accomplish as much as possible in a given amount of time (and probably always have a stopwatch or countdown timer running). It is much slower to read material with little contrast to its background. But I may also appreciate the aesthetics of my work environment, and the transparent mode is very pleasing to look at.

### Supporting information

System hardware state information on the Dock is very useful and glanced at many times per work session. Dock/Material/Transparent is very classy, especially with the text in black, but can be very difficult to read on darker backgrounds. Acrylic is very easy to read but not as elegant.

title: Appreciation for the PowerToys Command Palette documentation
author: gyodragos-cell
body:
### Describe the requested doc changes

I just wanted to say thank you for the amazing work on PowerToys Command Palette.  
The documentation is clear, inspiring, and shows how much effort and passion the authors put into it.  
It’s easy to understand, beautifully designed, and truly helps users accomplish their tasks.  
You’ve made something special — I hope this message makes your day brighter and brings you many likes, because you deserve it!

title: Enhanced Clipboard
author: otherwork07
body:
### Description of the new feature / enhancement

Upgrade the copy and paste (clipboard) functionality with advanced features such as: monitoring application actions on the clipboard, highlighting copied content with a slow-motion blinking effect to help users easily check if the desired content has been copied, etc.

It is possible to upgrade and rename "Advanced Paste" to "Enhanced Copy and Paste Functionality".

### Scenario when this would be used?

Upgrade the copy and paste (clipboard) functionality with advanced features such as: monitoring application actions on the clipboard, highlighting copied content with a slow-motion blinking effect to help users easily check if the desired content has been copied, etc.

### Supporting information

_No response_

title: Add comments to keyboard manager
author: rennefJ
body:
### Description of the new feature / enhancement

In the keyboard manager it would be great if there was an additional optional field where one could add comments to the individual keyboard shortcuts.


### Scenario when this would be used?

If you have a lot of keyboard shortcuts in keyboard manager, you loose track of which shortcut is for which action exactly or why one created that combination. 
Allowing to add comments to each keyboard shortcut would allow to mor easily figure out what the shortcut is for.
For example:
You have "Alt + T maps to Ctrl + T in msedge" -> Comment "Open new tab in Edge Browser"

### Supporting information

_No response_

title: CmdPal: Dock: Incorrectly reports display 1 as display 2
author: ReginaldTang
body: ### Microsoft PowerToys version 0.100.2 ### Installation method Microsoft Store ### Area(s) with issue? Command Palette ### Steps to reproduce 1. Connect a display to a device that has a 'lid' 2. Put the main device to sleep 3. While the device is sleeping, get the lock screen display on the second monitor (main device sleeping, second one active) (Would be easier on a device with an external power button: shut the lid first, and then press the button to wake up the external monitor) #### Would be more effective to reproduce if you wait ~10 seconds between closing the lid and pressing the power button ### ✔️ Expected Behavior CmdPal correctly displays the correct dock for each display ### ❌ Actual Behavior CmdPal mixes up the displays (thinks display 1(actual) is display 2(what it thinks)) ### Upload Bug Report ZIP-file _No response_ ### Additional Information _No response_ ### Other Software _No response_

title: Documentation: broken links and incorrect source file references in developer docs
author: Moli13337
body:
## Documentation: broken links and incorrect source file references

While reviewing the developer documentation, I found three files with broken links or incorrect source file references:

### 1. `doc/devdocs/modules/fileexploreraddons.md` — broken source code link

Line 17 links to `src/modules/fileexplorerpreview`, which does not exist in the repository. The actual source code directory is `src/modules/previewpane`.

### 2. `doc/devdocs/modules/launcher/plugins/calculator.md` — broken unit test links (10 occurrences)

Lines 50–63 link to `Microsoft.PowerToys.Run.Plugin.Calculator.UnitTests` (plural), but the actual test project directory is `Microsoft.PowerToys.Run.Plugin.Calculator.UnitTest` (singular). All 10 links in this section are broken.

### 3. `doc/devdocs/core/runner.md` — incorrect file reference

Line 122 references `centralized_keyboard_hook.cpp`, but the actual file is `centralized_kb_hook.cpp` (located at `src/runner/centralized_kb_hook.cpp`).

I will submit a PR to fix these.

title: Command palette dock is unstable with frequent crashes
author: azazyahmed
body:
### Microsoft PowerToys version

v0.100.2

### Installation method

Microsoft Store

### Area(s) with issue?

Command Palette

### Steps to reproduce

Usually Spontaneous
Sometimes, on adding new a new extension, or using an extension (particularly the Simple snippet & To-do list)


### ✔️ Expected Behavior

Command palette dock to be stable, and retain details/layouts with multiple screens

### ❌ Actual Behavior

Command palete dock is unstable with frequent crashes. 
Also, when working with multiple screens, the dock layouts (detail) are not retained correctly. Docks assigned to specific screens are often moved unexpectedly between monitors

Microsoft .NET Runtime - 5.0.17 (x64)
English
